title: Brain Lobes
subtitle: Learn the four major lobes of the brain and their key functions.

cards.frontPrompt: Which highlighted lobe helps with planning, personality and voluntary movement?
backPoints: Speech production in the dominant hemisphere
cards.memoryLine: Frontal lobe = the brain's organiser and decision-maker.

cards.frontPrompt: Which highlighted lobe processes touch and helps you understand where your body is?
cards.memoryLine: Parietal lobe = feeling your body and knowing where it is.

cards.frontPrompt: Which highlighted lobe is important for hearing, memory and understanding language?
cards.memoryLine: Temporal lobe = hearing, memories and meaning.

cards.frontPrompt: Which highlighted lobe processes visual information?
cards.memoryLine: Occipital lobe = the brain's visual processing centre.

cards.frontPrompt: Why can a problem on one side of the brain affect the opposite side of the body?
backPoints: Left brain affects the right body
Right brain affects the left body

questions.question: Which lobe is mainly responsible for vision?
questions.feedback: The occipital lobe processes visual information from the eyes.

questions.question: Which lobe is most associated with planning, personality and decision-making?
questions.feedback: The frontal lobe supports executive functions, behaviour and voluntary movement.

questions.question: Which lobe processes touch and body-position information?
questions.feedback: The parietal lobe processes sensation and helps with spatial and body awareness.

questions.question: Which lobe is important for hearing and memory?
questions.feedback: The temporal lobe is strongly involved in hearing, memory and understanding language.

questions.question: A patient develops poor judgement and a marked personality change. Which lobe may be affected?
questions.feedback: Frontal-lobe injury can affect judgement, behaviour, personality and planning.

questions.question: A patient suddenly loses vision on one side. Which lobe is most likely affected?
questions.feedback: The occipital lobe is responsible for processing vision.

questions.question: A stroke affects the left side of the brain. Which side of the body is most likely to become weak?
questions.feedback: Most movement pathways cross over, so the left side of the brain controls the right side of the body.
